channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: TNF alpha unmasks enteric malate aspartate shuttle dysfunction bridging Parkinson disease and intestinal inflammation.
item.description: Nature communications - Gastrointestinal dysfunction often precedes motor symptoms in Parkinson's disease (PD), suggesting the enteric nervous system (ENS) is central to early pathogenesis. How α-synuclein contributes to ...

item.title: Crohn's disease: research progress in decoding pathogenic multi-network and precision management of artificial intelligence radiomics.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic, relapsing inflammatory bowel disease characterized by transmural inflammation. Its clinical presentation and disease course are highly heterogeneous across indivi...

item.title: Investigating Handgrip Strength as a Practical Biomarker for Therapeutic Endpoints and Nutritional Status in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Current biomarkers for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) often rely on invasive procedures and lack broad availability, limiting their utility in clinical practice. This study aims to investigate th...

item.title: Evaluating the correlation between fecal and serum calprotectin in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Scientific reports -

item.title: Chlamydia trachomatis proctitis mimicking an inflammatory bowel disease and hiding human immunodeficiency virus infection.
item.description: European journal of clinical microbiology & infectious diseases : official publication of the European Society of Clinical Microbiology -

item.title: Efficacy and Safety of Ravagalimab in Patients with Moderate-to-Severe Ulcerative Colitis: Data from a Phase 2a Trial.
item.description: Advances in therapy - The monoclonal antibody ravagalimab is a potent antagonist to cluster of differentiation 40 (CD40), which is elevated in inflammatory diseases such as ulcerative colitis (UC). This phase 2a, open-l...

item.title: Evaluation of the effectiveness and safety of anti-CGRP monoclonal antibodies in patients with migraine and autoimmune diseases: IMMUNO-CGRP study.
item.description: Headache - This study aimed to evaluate demographic characteristics, treatment effectiveness, and safety outcomes in patients with migraine undergoing anti-calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) treatments re...

item.title: Adverse Events and Safety Outcomes Associated With Chemotherapy for Inflammatory Bowel Disease-associated Gastrointestinal Cancers.
item.description: Anticancer research - Management of inflammatory bowel disease-associated gastrointestinal cancers, including Crohn's disease-associated cancer and ulcerative colitis-associated cancer, remains challenging because of co...

item.title: Mesenteric and antimesenteric border subregionalization using MR enterography: advancing fibrosis evaluation in Crohn disease.
item.description: European radiology experimental - Assessment of intestinal fibrosis is an unmet need for patients with Crohn's disease (CD), but spatial heterogeneity of fibrosis may affect accuracy. We developed and validated a novel strategy bas...

item.title: Association between index adenomas and advanced findings at third colonoscopy: Data from the New Hampshire Colonoscopy Registry.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The United States Multi-Society Task Force for Colorectal Cancer (CRC) provides recommendations for the 2nd surveillance colonoscopy for patients with adenomas on index exam. However, evidence whic...

item.title: Use of magnetically controlled capsule endoscopy in inflammatory pediatric Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - This study aimed to evaluate the clinical utility of magnetically controlled capsule endoscopy (MCCE) in the evaluation and management of pediatric Crohn's disease (CD). A single-center retrospecti...

item.title: Twelve‑month rehospitalization after IBD‑related hospitalization in Germany: A retrospective cohort study using administrative hospital data.
item.description: PloS one - Multicenter study - Rehospitalization is an important indicator of disease burden and healthcare utilization in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet real-world data from Germany remain scarce. This study aimed to des...

item.title: Persistence of advanced therapies in patients with ulcerative colitis: real-world data from the nationwide CREdIT registry.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - The expanding therapeutic landscape of ulcerative colitis (UC) introduces uncertainty regarding the optimal positioning of new agents. This study aimed to compare the real-world persistence of adva...

item.title: Mortality trends from colorectal pathologies in the United States (1999-2020): a retrospective cohort study using CDC WONDER.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Colorectal diseases encompass both malignant and benign conditions with significant public health implications. While colorectal cancer (CRC) mortality has been well characterized, population-level...

item.title: Univariate- and machine learning-based plasma metabolite signature differentiates PSC-IBD from IBD and is predicted to be driven by gut microbial changes.
item.description: Metabolomics : Official journal of the Metabolomic Society - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a group of chronic inflammatory conditions of the gastrointestinal tract comprising two major phenotypes, Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Up to...

item.title: Gut-Brain Axis Dysregulation in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Implications for Coagulation Abnormalities and Extraintestinal Manifestations.
item.description: International journal of general medicine - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) involves chronic intestinal inflammation driven by gut-brain axis imbalance, fostering complications through an "inflammation-neuro-coagulation" triad. Current stag...

item.title: TNFSF13 insufficiency disrupts human colonic epithelial cell growth and associated B cell dynamics.
item.description: The Journal of clinical investigation - Cytokines mediating epithelial and immune cell interactions modulate mucosal healing-a process that goes awry with chronic inflammation as in inflammatory bowel disease. TNFSF13 is a cytokine impor...

item.title: Investigating the shared genetic architecture between inflammatory bowel disease and pancreatitis: a genome-wide cross-trait analysis.
item.description: Molecular genetics and genomics : MGG - Meta-analysis - Epidemiological studies suggested comorbidity between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and pancreatitis. However, little is known about their shared genetic architecture. Based on genome-wide assoc...

item.title: ORMDL3: from an asthma susceptibility gene to multi-disease associations.
item.description: Frontiers in genetics - Review - The ORMDL3 protein, encoded by the ORMDL3 gene, functions as a transmembrane protein in the endoplasmic reticulum. Initially identified through its genetic link to asthma susceptibility, ORMDL3 pla...

item.title: Factors associated with urgent consultation recommendations for patients with inflammatory bowel disease calling a telephone service about worsening symptoms: a secondary analysis.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology -
